summary: Trophy-list decomp/matching PR centered on src/melee/ty/tylist.c. It converted several raw trophy-list fields into typed arrays/struct fields, consolidated ToyGlobalsS_ into a shared header, matched five tylist functions, and replaced the un_80313774 placeholder with a partial C implementation. The decomp.dev bot reported +2552 matched bytes and new 100% matches for un_803124BC, un_8031263C, un_8031305C, un_80313508, and fn_8031438C; un_80313774 improved from 0% to 62.48%. There was no PR body or human review text in the slice.

key_files.role: Main matching work: archive-load string-offset changes, un_80313774 implementation, JObj helper refactor, TyListArg/TyListState field-use cleanup, fn_8031438C and un_803147C4 codegen tuning.
Refined TyListArg and TyListState layouts; added shared ToySubStructS_ and unified ToyGlobalsS_ layout.
Removed local partial ToyGlobals/ToySubStruct overlay structs and updated casts/locals to use the shared ToyGlobalsS_.
Updated un_8031305C prototype return type from s8 to s32.

what_changed: Replaced several local toy.c overlay structs, including ToyGlobals2S_, ToyGlobals3S_, ToyGlobals4S_, ToyGlobals5S_, ToySubStructS_, and TyUnk25, with a broader shared ToyGlobalsS_ and ToySubStructS_ in types.h.
Expanded ToyGlobalsS_ to cover offsets used by both toy.c and tylist.c, including x4, x10, x58, x140, x144, x148, x14C, x150, and x154.
Refactored TyListArg from raw fields x0/x4/x8/xC/x10/x14 plus padding into links[3], jobjs[3], texts[3], x25, x28, idx, x2C, and x30.
Updated tylist.c uses of list navigation from x0/x4 to links[0]/links[1], and JObj slots from xC/x10/x14 to jobjs[0]/jobjs[1]/jobjs[2].
Exposed TyListState.x2C2 in types.h and used it in fn_8031438C instead of byte-pointer indexing at offset 0x2C2.
Added a local TyListWaitData struct in tylist.c for offset-based writes through entry->x4, replacing raw ((s32*) tmp)[8] and [9] writes with x20 and x24.
Changed un_8031305C from s8 to s32 in tylist.h and tylist.c, adjusted stack padding to PAD_STACK(0xC), and introduced a comparison temporary used for matching.

Replaced direct string literals and named string symbols in un_803124BC and un_8031263C archive loading with offsets from char* data = un_803FDD18, such as data + 0x608, data + 0x614, and symbol-name offsets near 0x9DC-0xA74.
Refactored un_80313508 to call HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z instead of manually assigning translate fields and reproducing dirty-matrix flag logic.

Implemented un_80313774, replacing the previous placeholder with code that initializes trophy-list state, creates circular TyListArg links, loads list entry jobjs/text objects, creates cursor/marker jobjs, advances initial list position, and calls un_80312BAC.
Adjusted fn_8031438C to use TyListArchive jobjs array access, typed state->x2C2, TyListWaitData, an HSD_Text* temporary, and a double-cast assignment for default_alignment.
Adjusted un_803147C4 to use char* base-pointer arithmetic for state memory, a HSD_GObj** pointer for gobj_2C4, and raw memzero offsets where that matched better.
smart_moves: The TyListArg array refactor captured repeated structure at fixed offsets: links[0]/links[1] made circular list behavior explicit, jobjs[0..2] grouped the JObj triplet, and texts[0..2] turned former padding into meaningful HSD_Text pointers.
Unifying ToyGlobalsS_ avoided multiple local partial overlay types for the same global object and made shared trophy/toy offsets available from types.h.
Using HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z in un_80313508 avoided hand-inlining library dirty-flag behavior and produced a cleaner full match.

The archive string-offset tactic matched functions that previously used normal literals, showing that the original code likely referenced a shared string/data block rather than standalone string symbols.
Adding TyListWaitData converted opaque indexed stores into a small typed layout without needing to name the whole pointed-to object.
The PR mixed semantic cleanup with exact-codegen choices: it exposed x2C2 as a field where safe, but kept raw data-pointer memzero patterns in un_803147C4 where the compiler shape needed it.
decomp_lessons: When several partial structs describe the same global allocation, prefer one wide shared layout with unknown fields left as xNN/padNN; it reduces contradictory casts and helps later functions share discoveries.
For repeated same-type fields, arrays can improve both readability and matching while preserving exact offsets, especially for ring-list pointers, JObj slots, and text handles.
Do not assume direct field writes are the best match for HSD objects; library setters may match the original compiler output and hide complex dirty-flag behavior.
String references can be a codegen lever. If direct literals or named global strings fail, try using a char* base plus constant offsets into the known string/data blob.
Prototype return types affect matching. un_8031305C needed s32 rather than s8, plus matching stack padding, to reach 100%.
It is acceptable for a PR to improve function matching while temporarily regressing data-section matching; here the bot reported five new function matches but a .data regression.
naming_conventions: TyListArg.links[0] and links[1] replaced x0 and x4 for circular neighbor traversal; links[2] preserves the third pointer slot formerly at x8.
TyListArg.jobjs[0], jobjs[1], and jobjs[2] replaced xC, x10, and x14 for the JObj slots.
TyListArg.texts[0..2] names the three HSD_Text* slots at offsets 0x18, 0x1C, and 0x20.
Unknown but verified offset fields stayed in xNN style, such as TyListState.x2C2, ToyGlobalsS_.x58, ToyGlobalsS_.x140, and TyListWaitData.x20/x24.
ToyGlobalsS_ became the shared type name for a broader toy/trophy globals layout; smaller overlay names ToyGlobals2S_/3S_/4S_/5S_ and TyUnk25 were removed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use char* data = un_803FDD18 and pass data + fixed offsets to lbArchive_LoadSymbols when matching tylist archive-name/symbol-name calls.
For un_8031305C, s32 return type, PAD_STACK(0xC), and storing the x29F comparison in i were part of the final matched shape.
For un_80313508, structure the null-joint case as OSPanic followed by an else block, then use HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z and return either the newly added child or a GObj-created object.
For fn_8031438C, typed array access archive->jobjs[i], a local HSD_Text* temporary, and assigning default_alignment as (double) 2 helped reach 100%.
For un_803147C4, raw base-pointer memory operations such as memzero(data + 0x2AC, 0x18), memzero(gobj, 0x14), and memzero(data, 0x2AC) were retained for codegen.
The un_80313774 implementation sets up TyListArg entries as a circular list, advances entry = entry->links[1], and uses un_8031305C in a loop to align initial state.
review_feedback: No human review comments and no PR body were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01 c14d251..0bdec7e: matched code increased to 67.24%, +0.07%, +2552 bytes.
Bot reported 5 new matches: un_8031263C, fn_8031438C, un_8031305C, un_80313508, and un_803124BC all reached 100%.
Bot reported unmatched improvements: un_80313774 improved from 0.00% to 62.48%, .sdata2 from 12.90% to 37.84%, un_803147C4 from 78.58% to 85.97%, and fn_80313BD8 from 95.22% to 95.34%.
Bot reported one regression: tylist .data dropped from 20.07% to 4.40%.
follow_up_queries: Find later tylist PRs that finish un_80313774 after this 62.48% implementation.
Search for TyListArg.links[2] and TyListArg.x25 to determine whether the current names should become more semantic.
Check whether the .data regression from replacing literals with un_803FDD18 offsets was later recovered.
Look for remaining ToyGlobals partial overlays outside toy.c that can be replaced with the shared ToyGlobalsS_ layout.
Continue refining TyListState around offsets 0x2B8-0x2D8 now that x2C2 and gobj_2C4 are exposed.
